prinsurf 2.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

    Gradient-flow biplot axis for one variable

    psaxis.Rd

    Constructs the calibrated gradient-flow axis for a single variable of a fitted principal surface: the steepest-ascent trajectory of the variable's fitted coordinate function, started from the centroid of the sample coordinates. The axis is smooth, crosses the variable's contours orthogonally, and carries a monotone calibration. A variable is deferred (no axis) when its fitted value has an interior extremum beyond the support boundary (Criterion 1) or when the constructed axis spans too little of the variable's range (Criterion 2); such variables are read from contour lines instead.

    psaxis(
  object,
  var,
  N = 55,
  h = 0.03,
  steps = 800,
  delta = 0.05,
  cover_min = 0.55,
  defer = TRUE
)

    Arguments

    object

    A "prinsurf" object.

    var

    Variable name or column index.

    N

    Grid resolution for evaluating the coordinate function and gradient.

    h

    Integration step for the gradient flow.

    steps

    Maximum number of integration steps in each direction.

    delta

    Boundary-extremum margin, as a fraction of the value range.

    cover_min

    Minimum fraction of the variable's range the axis must span.

    defer

    Logical; if FALSE, deferral is switched off and a calibrated axis is returned for every variable that yields a non-degenerate flow (Criteria 1 and 2 are not applied).

    Value

    An object of class "psaxis": a list with axis (an \(m \times 2\) matrix of axis vertices, or NULL if deferred), cal (the calibration value at each vertex), monotone (logical; FALSE when deferred), coverage, var, and the evaluation grid g1, g2, M used for drawing contours.

    Examples

    set.seed(1)
s <- runif(120, -1, 1); t <- runif(120, -1, 1)
X <- cbind(x = s, y = t, z = 0.7 * s + 0.9 * t^2) +
     matrix(rnorm(360, 0, 0.03), 120, 3)
fit <- prinsurf(X, max.iter = 6)
#>   iter 1  rel.change 0.96112  sumD 0.834
#>   iter 2  rel.change 0.10532  sumD 0.746
#>   iter 3  rel.change 0.05802  sumD 0.702
#>   iter 4  rel.change 0.02196  sumD 0.687
#>   iter 5  rel.change 0.00636  sumD 0.683
#>   iter 6  rel.change 0.01024  sumD 0.690
ax <- psaxis(fit, "z")
ax$monotone
#> [1] TRUE
